solution:
def hasAlternatingBits(self, n: int) -> bool:
    return ((n & (n - 1)) == 0)

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution checks if the bitwise AND of n with (n - 1) is equal to 0. This condition represents that all the bits of n are either 0 or 1, which means they have alternating values. The time complexity is O(1) because the operation is constant time, and the space complexity is O(1) because the function doesn't use any additional memory. The energy implications are low because the operation doesn't consume significant energy.
